Introduction

Who I Am

I'm Pranav Ramanatha Siva — a Cybersecurity Analytics and Operations student at Pennsylvania State University, originally from Singapore. My academic journey is fueled by a deep passion for understanding how digital systems work, and more importantly, how to keep them secure.

Beyond the classroom, I bring a rare combination of technical rigor and real-world leadership. As a former national cricketer who represented Singapore in the ICC World Cup Qualifiers, I've learned to perform under pressure, think strategically, and lead teams toward a common goal — skills that transfer seamlessly into the cybersecurity world.

I've completed internships in cyber security, worked as an IT Service Specialist at Penn State, and hold certifications in Linux, Pentesting, Networking, and more. I'm passionate about applying cybersecurity best practices to protect real systems, and I'm always looking to grow, connect, and contribute.

Coursework

Assignments & Projects

This section includes my major IST 110 coursework, excluding quizzes and reading checks. Each assignment helped me build a stronger understanding of information, people, technology, cybersecurity, data, and digital systems.

01
Portfolio Planning
Semester Learning and Portfolio Development Plan
Created an early plan for tracking my learning, organizing course work, and building a final portfolio that shows my growth throughout IST 110.
Reflection: This helped me think about the portfolio as more than a final website. It became a record of what I learned and how my skills developed during the semester.
02
Course Setup
Enterprise Software Setup
Set up the enterprise tools needed for the course and learned how cloud-based platforms support communication, collaboration, and productivity.
Reflection: This connected directly to real workplace technology because many organizations depend on these tools to keep teams organized and connected.
03
Collaboration
Group Collaboration Discussion
Reflected on how group work depends on communication, shared responsibility, and respectful collaboration.
Reflection: I learned that successful technology work is not only technical. It also depends on how well people communicate and support each other.
04
IST Foundations
Introduce Yourself and IT Interests
Introduced my background, interests, and goals in information science and technology.
Reflection: This assignment helped me connect my personal interests in cybersecurity and technology to the larger goals of the class.
05
Information Search
Virtual Information Treasure Hunt
Used digital research skills to locate, evaluate, and organize information from different sources.
Reflection: This showed me how important it is to find reliable information instead of simply accepting the first result online.
06
Computing Basics
Binary Representation
Explored how computers represent information using binary and how data can be translated into a form machines understand.
Reflection: This made computing feel more concrete because I could see how simple 0s and 1s support more complex digital systems.
07
Data Analytics
Data Analytics Discussion
Discussed how data can be collected, analyzed, and used to support better decisions.
Reflection: I learned that data is powerful, but it needs context and responsibility to be useful.
08
Computational Thinking
Computational Problem-Solving Infographic
Created an infographic explaining decomposition, pattern recognition, algorithms, and abstraction.
Reflection: Making the infographic helped me break down problem solving into clear steps that can be applied in coding, cybersecurity, and everyday decisions.
09
Hardware
Hardware Discussion
Explored the role of computer hardware and how physical components support digital systems.
Reflection: This reminded me that every digital tool depends on real physical parts working together correctly.
10
Hardware
Hardware Dissection Lab
Studied computer components and identified how hardware pieces connect to create a functioning device.
Reflection: This lab helped me understand computers from the inside, not just as devices I use from the outside.
11
Operating Systems
Operating System Discussion
Discussed the purpose of operating systems and how they manage hardware, software, files, and user interaction.
Reflection: I learned that operating systems are the bridge between the user and the machine.
12
Operating Systems
Choose the Best OS
Compared operating systems and evaluated which one would work best for different users or situations.
Reflection: This assignment showed me that the best technology choice depends on the user's goals, comfort level, and needs.
13
Programming
Programming Languages Discussion
Reflected on different programming languages and how they are used to solve problems.
Reflection: This helped me see that programming is not just about syntax. It is about choosing the right tool for the task.
14
Creative Tech
Suno Song Midterm
Used an AI music tool to create a song and think about how emerging technology can support creativity.
Reflection: This was a fun reminder that technology is not only technical. It can also be creative and expressive.
15
Python
Python Programming
Practiced programming logic, syntax, and problem solving through Python activities.
Reflection: Python helped me understand how instructions become actions. It also strengthened my confidence with coding basics.
16
Data Organization
Data Organization Discussion
Discussed how data is structured, stored, and used across different systems.
Reflection: I learned that organized data makes technology more useful, searchable, and reliable.
17
Databases
CODIS Cold Case
Examined how DNA databases can help solve cold cases while also raising questions about privacy and ethics.
Reflection: This assignment helped me see both sides of data use: it can solve serious problems, but it must be handled carefully.
18
Data Systems
Music Genome Project
Explored how music recommendation systems classify songs and use data to personalize experiences.
Reflection: This showed me how data organization affects everyday platforms like music apps and recommendation systems.
19
SQL
SQL Assignment
Practiced using SQL to query, filter, and retrieve information from structured databases.
Reflection: SQL made databases feel practical because I could directly ask questions and get useful results from stored data.
20
Applications
OSINT Activity
Explored open-source intelligence and learned how publicly available information can be collected and analyzed.
Reflection: This activity connected strongly to cybersecurity because it showed how much information can be discovered online.
21
Internet and Web
Internet and Web Discussion
Discussed the structure of the internet, web technologies, and how online communication works.
Reflection: This helped me understand the internet as a system built from protocols, infrastructure, and user interaction.
22
Web Development
HTML Assignment
Built web page structure using HTML and learned how content is organized for the browser.
Reflection: HTML gave me the foundation for this portfolio because it showed me how a webpage is actually built.
23
Web Development
JavaScript Assignment
Used JavaScript to add interactivity and logic to a webpage.
Reflection: This assignment showed me how websites can respond to users instead of only displaying static information.
24
Security
Security Concept Infographic
Created a visual explanation of a cybersecurity concept and communicated it in a clear, simple way.
Reflection: This helped me practice explaining security topics to people who may not have a technical background.
25
Privacy
Database Technology and Privacy Issues Discussion
Discussed how databases collect, store, and connect personal information, along with the privacy risks that come with that power.
Reflection: This made me think more carefully about how convenient digital systems can also create privacy concerns.
26
Privacy
What Do Companies Know About You?
Investigated what kinds of data companies can collect about users and how that information may be used.
Reflection: This assignment made privacy feel personal because it showed how much data is connected to everyday online activity.
27
Society and Technology
L12 Postcard
Created a short visual message connected to technology's impact on society.
Reflection: This helped me practice communicating a technology-related idea in a concise and visual format.
28
Cybercrime
Cybercrime Assignment
Explored forms of cybercrime such as hacking, identity theft, malware, extortion, and data breaches.
Reflection: This assignment connected directly to my cybersecurity interests and showed why digital safety matters for individuals, businesses, and society.
29
Business Technology
Crowdsourcing Discussion
Discussed how businesses and communities use crowdsourcing to gather ideas, solve problems, and create value.
Reflection: I learned that technology can bring many people together to contribute knowledge, creativity, and solutions.
30
Enterprise Technology
Enterprise Technology Integration Assignment
Explored how organizations connect enterprise systems to improve communication, productivity, and decision-making.
Reflection: This helped me understand how workplace technology must work together smoothly for an organization to function well.
31
Final Portfolio
Learning Portfolio Milestones and Final Site
Developed this portfolio through milestones, feedback, revisions, and final improvements.
Reflection: Building the portfolio helped me pull the whole course together. It shows not only what I completed, but also what I learned about technology and myself.
